1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   module Core
5:     module Importer
6:       class Order
7:         def self.import(user, params)
8:           params = params.to_h
9:           ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
10:             ensure_country_id_from_params params[:ship_address_attributes]
11:             ensure_state_id_from_params params[:ship_address_attributes]
12:             ensure_country_id_from_params params[:bill_address_attributes]
13:             ensure_state_id_from_params params[:bill_address_attributes]
14: 
15:             create_params = params.slice :currency
16:             order = Spree::Order.create! create_params
17:             order.store ||= Spree::Store.default
18:             order.associate_user!(user)
19:             order.save!
20: 
21:             shipments_attrs = params.delete(:shipments_attributes)
22: 
23:             create_line_items_from_params(params.delete(:line_items_attributes), order)
24:             create_shipments_from_params(shipments_attrs, order)
25:             create_adjustments_from_params(params.delete(:adjustments_attributes), order)
26:             create_payments_from_params(params.delete(:payments_attributes), order)
27: 
28:             params.delete(:user_id) unless user.try(:has_spree_role?, "admin") && params.key?(:user_id)
29: 
30:             completed_at = params.delete(:completed_at)
31: 
32:             order.update!(params)
33: 
34:             order.create_proposed_shipments unless shipments_attrs.present?
35: 
36:             if completed_at
37:               order.completed_at = completed_at
38:               order.state = "complete"
39:               order.save!
40:             end
41: 
42:             # Really ensure that the order totals & states are correct
43:             order.recalculate
44:             if shipments_attrs.present?
45:               order.shipments.each_with_index do |shipment, index|
46:                 shipment.update_columns(cost: shipments_attrs[index][:cost].to_f) if shipments_attrs[index][:cost].present?
47:               end
48:             end
49:             order.reload
50:           end
51:         end
52: 
53:         def self.create_shipments_from_params(shipments_hash, order)
54:           return [] unless shipments_hash
55: 
56:           shipments_hash.each do |target|
57:             shipment = Shipment.new
58:             shipment.tracking = target[:tracking]
59:             shipment.stock_location = Spree::StockLocation.find_by(id: target[:stock_location_id]) ||
60:               Spree::StockLocation.find_by(admin_name: target[:stock_location]) ||
61:               Spree::StockLocation.find_by!(name: target[:stock_location])
62: 
63:             inventory_units = target[:inventory_units] || []
64:             inventory_units.each do |inventory_unit|
65:               ensure_variant_id_from_params(inventory_unit)
66: 
67:               unless (line_item = order.line_items.find_by(variant_id: inventory_unit[:variant_id]))
68:                 line_item = order.contents.add(Spree::Variant.find(inventory_unit[:variant_id]), 1)
69:               end
70: 
71:               # Spree expects a Inventory Unit to always reference a line
72:               # item and variant otherwise users might get exceptions when
73:               # trying to view these units. Note the Importer might not be
74:               # able to find the line item if line_item.variant_id |= iu.variant_id
75:               shipment.inventory_units.new(
76:                 variant_id: inventory_unit[:variant_id],
77:                 line_item:
78:               )
79:             end
80: 
81:             # Mark shipped if it should be.
82:             if target[:shipped_at].present?
83:               shipment.shipped_at = target[:shipped_at]
84:               shipment.state = "shipped"
85:               shipment.inventory_units.each do |unit|
86:                 unit.state = "shipped"
87:               end
88:             end
89: 
90:             order.shipments << shipment
91:             shipment.save!
92: 
93:             shipping_method = Spree::ShippingMethod.find_by(name: target[:shipping_method]) || Spree::ShippingMethod.find_by!(admin_name: target[:shipping_method])
94:             rate = shipment.shipping_rates.create!(shipping_method:,
95:               cost: target[:cost])
96:             shipment.selected_shipping_rate_id = rate.id
97:             shipment.update_amounts
98:           end
99:         end
100: 
101:         def self.create_line_items_from_params(line_items_hash, order)
102:           return {} unless line_items_hash
103: 
104:           case line_items_hash
105:           when Hash
106:             line_items_hash.each_key do |key|
107:               create_line_item(line_items_hash[key], order)
108:             end
109:           when Array
110:             line_items_hash.each do |line_item_hash|
111:               create_line_item(line_item_hash, order)
112:             end
113:           end
114:         end
115: 
116:         def self.create_line_item(line_item_hash, order)
117:           extra_params = line_item_hash.except(:variant_id, :quantity, :sku)
118:           line_item = ensure_variant_id_from_params(line_item_hash)
119:           line_item = order.contents.add(Spree::Variant.find(line_item[:variant_id]), line_item[:quantity])
120:           # Raise any errors with saving to prevent import succeeding with line items failing silently.
121:           if extra_params.present?
122:             line_item.update!(extra_params)
123:           else
124:             line_item.save!
125:           end
126:         end
127: 
128:         def self.create_adjustments_from_params(adjustments, order)
129:           return [] unless adjustments
130:           adjustments.each do |target|
131:             adjustment = order.adjustments.build(
132:               order:,
133:               amount: target[:amount].to_d,
134:               label: target[:label]
135:             )
136:             adjustment.save!
137:             adjustment.finalize!
138:           end
139:         end
140: 
141:         def self.create_payments_from_params(payments_hash, order)
142:           return [] unless payments_hash
143:           payments_hash.each do |target|
144:             payment = order.payments.build(order:)
145:             payment.amount = target[:amount].to_f
146:             # Order API should be using state as that's the normal payment field.
147:             # spree_wombat serializes payment state as status so imported orders should fall back to status field.
148:             payment.state = target[:state] || target[:status] || "completed"
149:             payment.payment_method = Spree::PaymentMethod.find_by!(name: target[:payment_method])
150:             source_attributes = target[:source] || target[:source_attributes]
151:             payment.source = create_source_payment_from_params(source_attributes, payment) if source_attributes
152:             payment.save!
153:           end
154:         end
155: 
156:         def self.create_source_payment_from_params(source_hash, payment)
157:           Spree::CreditCard.create(
158:             month: source_hash[:month],
159:             year: source_hash[:year],
160:             cc_type: source_hash[:cc_type],
161:             last_digits: source_hash[:last_digits],
162:             name: source_hash[:name],
163:             payment_method: payment.payment_method,
164:             gateway_customer_profile_id: source_hash[:gateway_customer_profile_id],
165:             gateway_payment_profile_id: source_hash[:gateway_payment_profile_id],
166:             imported: true
167:           )
168:         end
169: 
170:         def self.ensure_variant_id_from_params(hash)
171:           sku = hash.delete(:sku)
172:           unless hash[:variant_id].present?
173:             hash[:variant_id] = Spree::Variant.with_prices.find_by!(sku:).id
174:           end
175:           hash
176:         end
177: 
178:         def self.ensure_country_id_from_params(address)
179:           return if address.nil? || address[:country_id].present? || address[:country].nil?
180: 
181:           search = {}
182:           if (name = address[:country]["name"])
183:             search[:name] = name
184:           elsif (iso_name = address[:country]["iso_name"])
185:             search[:iso_name] = iso_name.upcase
186:           elsif (iso = address[:country]["iso"])
187:             search[:iso] = iso.upcase
188:           elsif (iso_three = address[:country]["iso3"])
189:             search[:iso3] = iso_three.upcase
190:           end
191: 
192:           address.delete(:country)
193:           address[:country_id] = Spree::Country.where(search).first!.id
194:         end
195: 
196:         def self.ensure_state_id_from_params(address)
197:           return if address.nil? || address[:state_id].present? || address[:state].nil?
198: 
199:           search = {}
200:           if (name = address[:state]["name"])
201:             search[:name] = name
202:           elsif (abbr = address[:state]["abbr"])
203:             search[:abbr] = abbr.upcase
204:           end
205: 
206:           address.delete(:state)
207:           search[:country_id] = address[:country_id]
208: 
209:           if (state = Spree::State.where(search).first)
210:             address[:state_id] = state.id
211:           else
212:             address[:state_name] = search[:name] || search[:abbr]
213:           end
214:         end
215:       end
216:     end
217:   end
218: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Service object for creating new payments on an Order
5:   class PaymentCreate
6:     # @param order [Order] The order for the new payment
7:     # @param attributes [Hash,ActionController::Parameters] attributes which are assigned to the new payment
8:     #   * :payment_method_id Id of payment method used for this payment
9:     #   * :source_attributes Attributes used to build the source of this payment. Usually a {CreditCard}
10:     #     * :wallet_payment_source_id (Integer): The id of a {WalletPaymentSource} to use
11:     # @param request_env [Hash] rack env of user creating the payment
12:     # @param payment [Payment] Internal use only. Instead of making a new payment, change the attributes for an existing one.
13:     def initialize(order, attributes, payment: nil, request_env: {})
14:       @order = order
15:       @payment = payment
16: 
17:       # If AC::Params are passed in, attributes.to_h gives us a hash of only
18:       # the permitted attributes.
19:       @attributes = attributes.to_h.with_indifferent_access
20:       @source_attributes = @attributes.delete(:source_attributes) || {}
21:       @request_env = request_env
22:     end
23: 
24:     # Build the new Payment
25:     # @return [Payment] a new (unpersisted) Payment
26:     def build
27:       @payment ||= order.payments.new
28:       @payment.request_env = @request_env if @request_env
29:       @payment.attributes = @attributes
30: 
31:       if source_attributes[:wallet_payment_source_id].present?
32:         build_from_wallet_payment_source
33:       else
34:         build_source
35:       end
36: 
37:       @payment
38:     end
39: 
40:     private
41: 
42:     attr_reader :order, :payment, :attributes, :source_attributes
43: 
44:     def build_source
45:       payment_method = payment.payment_method
46:       if source_attributes.present? && payment_method.try(:payment_source_class)
47:         payment.source = payment_method.payment_source_class.new(source_attributes)
48:         payment.source.payment_method_id = payment_method.id
49:         if order && payment.source.respond_to?(:user=)
50:           payment.source.user = order.user
51:         end
52:       end
53:     end
54: 
55:     def build_from_wallet_payment_source
56:       wallet_payment_source_id = source_attributes.fetch(:wallet_payment_source_id)
57:       raise(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound) if order.user.nil?
58:       wallet_payment_source = order.user.wallet.find(wallet_payment_source_id)
59:       raise(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound) if wallet_payment_source.nil?
60:       build_from_payment_source(wallet_payment_source.payment_source)
61:     end
62: 
63:     def build_from_payment_source(payment_source)
64:       # FIXME: does this work?
65:       if source_attributes[:verification_value]
66:         payment_source.verification_value = source_attributes[:verification_value]
67:       end
68: 
69:       payment.source = payment_source
70:       payment.payment_method_id = payment_source.payment_method_id
71:     end
72: 
73:     def available_cards
74:       if (user_id = order.user_id)
75:         Spree::CreditCard.where(user_id:)
76:       else
77:         Spree::CreditCard.none
78:       end
79:     end
80:   end
81: end

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   class Reimbursement < Spree::Base
5:     class IncompleteReimbursementError < StandardError; end
6: 
7:     belongs_to :order, inverse_of: :reimbursements, optional: true
8:     belongs_to :customer_return, inverse_of: :reimbursements, touch: true, optional: true
9: 
10:     has_many :refunds, inverse_of: :reimbursement
11:     has_many :credits, inverse_of: :reimbursement, class_name: "Spree::Reimbursement::Credit"
12: 
13:     has_many :return_items, inverse_of: :reimbursement
14: 
15:     validates :order, presence: true
16:     validate :validate_return_items_belong_to_same_order
17: 
18:     accepts_nested_attributes_for :return_items, allow_destroy: true
19: 
20:     before_create :generate_number
21:     before_create :calculate_total
22: 
23:     scope :reimbursed, -> { where(reimbursement_status: "reimbursed") }
24: 
25:     # The reimbursement_tax_calculator property should be set to an object that responds to "call"
26:     # and accepts a reimbursement object. Invoking "call" should update the tax fields on the
27:     # associated ReturnItems.
28:     # This allows a store to easily integrate with third party tax services.
29:     class_attribute :reimbursement_tax_calculator
30:     self.reimbursement_tax_calculator = ReimbursementTaxCalculator
31:     # A separate attribute here allows you to use a more performant calculator for estimates
32:     # and a different one (e.g. one that hits a 3rd party API) for the final caluclations.
33:     class_attribute :reimbursement_simulator_tax_calculator
34:     self.reimbursement_simulator_tax_calculator = ReimbursementTaxCalculator
35: 
36:     # The reimbursement_models property should contain an array of all models that provide
37:     # reimbursements.
38:     # This allows a store to incorporate custom reimbursement methods that Spree doesn't know about.
39:     # Each model must implement a "total_amount_reimbursed_for" method.
40:     # Example:
41:     #   Refund.total_amount_reimbursed_for(reimbursement)
42:     # See the `reimbursement_generator` property regarding the generation of custom reimbursements.
43:     class_attribute :reimbursement_models
44:     self.reimbursement_models = [Spree::Refund, Spree::Reimbursement::Credit]
45: 
46:     # The reimbursement_performer property should be set to an object that responds to the following methods:
47:     # - #perform
48:     # - #simulate
49:     # see ReimbursementPerformer for details.
50:     # This allows a store to customize their reimbursement methods and logic.
51:     class_attribute :reimbursement_performer
52:     self.reimbursement_performer = ReimbursementPerformer
53: 
54:     include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.reimbursement
55: 
56:     class << self
57:       def build_from_customer_return(customer_return)
58:         order = customer_return.order
59:         order.reimbursements.build({
60:           customer_return:,
61:           return_items: customer_return.return_items.accepted.not_reimbursed
62:         })
63:       end
64:     end
65: 
66:     def display_total
67:       Spree::Money.new(total, {currency: order.currency})
68:     end
69: 
70:     def calculated_total
71:       # rounding down to handle edge cases for consecutive partial returns where rounding
72:       # might cause us to try to reimburse more than was originally billed
73:       return_items.to_a.sum(&:total).to_d.round(2, :down)
74:     end
75: 
76:     def paid_amount
77:       reimbursement_models.sum do |model|
78:         model.total_amount_reimbursed_for(self)
79:       end
80:     end
81: 
82:     def unpaid_amount
83:       total - paid_amount
84:     end
85: 
86:     def perform!(created_by:)
87:       reimbursement_tax_calculator.call(self)
88:       reload
89:       update!(total: calculated_total)
90: 
91:       reimbursement_performer.perform(self, created_by:)
92: 
93:       if unpaid_amount_within_tolerance?
94:         reimbursed!
95:         Spree::Bus.publish :reimbursement_reimbursed, reimbursement: self
96:       else
97:         errored!
98:         Spree::Bus.publish :reimbursement_errored, reimbursement: self
99:       end
100: 
101:       if errored?
102:         raise IncompleteReimbursementError, I18n.t("spree.validation.unpaid_amount_not_zero", amount: unpaid_amount)
103:       end
104:     end
105: 
106:     def simulate(created_by:)
107:       reimbursement_simulator_tax_calculator.call(self)
108:       reload
109:       update!(total: calculated_total)
110: 
111:       reimbursement_performer.simulate(self, created_by:)
112:     end
113: 
114:     def return_items_requiring_exchange
115:       return_items.select(&:exchange_required?)
116:     end
117: 
118:     def any_exchanges?
119:       return_items.any?(&:exchange_processed?)
120:     end
121: 
122:     def all_exchanges?
123:       return_items.all?(&:exchange_processed?)
124:     end
125: 
126:     # Accepts all return items, saves the reimbursement, and performs the reimbursement
127:     #
128:     # @api public
129:     # @param [Spree.user_class] created_by the user that is performing this action
130:     # @return [void]
131:     def return_all(created_by:)
132:       return_items.each(&:accept!)
133:       save!
134:       perform!(created_by:)
135:     end
136: 
137:     # The returned category is used as the category
138:     # for Spree::Reimbursement::Credit.default_creditable_class.
139:     #
140:     # @return [Spree::StoreCreditCategory]
141:     def store_credit_category
142:       Spree::StoreCreditCategory.find_by(name: Spree::StoreCreditCategory::REIMBURSEMENT)
143:     end
144: 
145:     private
146: 
147:     def calculate_total
148:       self.total ||= calculated_total
149:     end
150: 
151:     def generate_number
152:       self.number ||= loop do
153:         random = "RI#{Array.new(9) { rand(9) }.join}"
154:         break random unless self.class.exists?(number: random)
155:       end
156:     end
157: 
158:     def validate_return_items_belong_to_same_order
159:       if return_items.any? { |ri| ri.inventory_unit.order_id != order_id }
160:         errors.add(:base, :return_items_order_id_does_not_match)
161:       end
162:     end
163: 
164:     # If there are multiple different reimbursement types for a single
165:     # reimbursement we open ourselves to a one-cent rounding error for every
166:     # type over the first one. This is due to how we round #unpaid_amount and
167:     # how each reimbursement type will round as well. Since at this point the
168:     # payments and credits have already been processed, we should allow the
169:     # reimbursement to show as 'reimbursed' and not 'errored'.
170:     def unpaid_amount_within_tolerance?
171:       reimbursement_count = reimbursement_models.count do |model|
172:         model.total_amount_reimbursed_for(self) > 0
173:       end
174:       leniency = if reimbursement_count > 0
175:         (reimbursement_count - 1) * BigDecimal("0.01")
176:       else
177:         0
178:       end
179:       unpaid_amount.abs.between?(0, leniency)
180:     end
181:   end
182: end

(End of file - total 182 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   class Exchange
5:     class UnableToCreateShipments < StandardError; end
6:     extend ActiveModel::Naming
7: 
8:     def initialize(order, reimbursement_objects)
9:       @order = order
10:       @reimbursement_objects = reimbursement_objects
11:     end
12: 
13:     def description
14:       @reimbursement_objects.map do |reimbursement_object|
15:         "#{reimbursement_object.variant.options_text} => #{reimbursement_object.exchange_variant.options_text}"
16:       end.join(" | ")
17:     end
18: 
19:     def display_amount
20:       Spree::Money.new @reimbursement_objects.sum(&:total)
21:     end
22: 
23:     def perform!
24:       begin
25:         shipments = Spree::Config.stock.coordinator_class.new(
26:           @order,
27:           inventory_units: @reimbursement_objects.map(&:build_exchange_inventory_unit)
28:         ).shipments
29:       rescue Spree::Order::InsufficientStock
30:         raise UnableToCreateShipments.new("Could not generate shipments for all items. Out of stock?")
31:       end
32:       @order.shipments += shipments
33:       @order.save!
34:       shipments.each do |shipment|
35:         shipment.update_state
36:         shipment.finalize!
37:       end
38:     end
39: 
40:     def to_key
41:       nil
42:     end
43: 
44:     def self.param_key
45:       "spree_exchange"
46:     end
47:   end
48: end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Variants placed in the Order at a particular price.
5:   #
6:   # `Spree::LineItem` is an ActiveRecord model which records which `Spree::Variant`
7:   # a customer has chosen to place in their order. It also acts as the permanent
8:   # record of the customer's order by recording relevant price, taxation, and inventory
9:   # concerns. Line items can also have adjustments placed on them as part of the
10:   # promotion system.
11:   #
12:   class LineItem < Spree::Base
13:     include Metadata
14: 
15:     belongs_to :order, class_name: "Spree::Order", inverse_of: :line_items, touch: true, optional: true
16:     belongs_to :variant, -> { with_discarded }, class_name: "Spree::Variant", inverse_of: :line_items, optional: true
17:     belongs_to :tax_category, class_name: "Spree::TaxCategory", optional: true
18: 
19:     has_one :product, through: :variant
20: 
21:     has_many :adjustments, as: :adjustable, inverse_of: :adjustable, dependent: :destroy, autosave: true
22:     has_many :inventory_units, inverse_of: :line_item
23: 
24:     before_validation :normalize_quantity
25:     after_initialize :set_required_attributes
26: 
27:     validates :variant, presence: true
28:     validates :quantity, numericality: {
29:       only_integer: true,
30:       greater_than: -1
31:     }
32:     validates :price, numericality: true
33:     validate :price_match_order_currency
34: 
35:     after_save :update_inventory
36: 
37:     before_destroy :update_inventory
38:     before_destroy :destroy_inventory_units
39: 
40:     delegate :name, :description, :sku, :should_track_inventory?, to: :variant
41:     delegate :tax_category, :tax_category_id, to: :variant, prefix: true
42:     delegate :currency, to: :order, allow_nil: true
43: 
44:     attr_accessor :target_shipment, :price_currency
45: 
46:     self.allowed_ransackable_associations = ["variant"]
47:     self.allowed_ransackable_attributes = ["variant_id"]
48: 
49:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this line item, which is the line
50:     #   item's price multiplied by its quantity.
51:     def amount
52:       price * quantity
53:     end
54:     alias_method :subtotal, :amount
55: 
56:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this line item, taking into
57:     #   consideration all its adjustments.
58:     def total
59:       amount + adjustment_total
60:     end
61: 
62:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this item, taking into consideration
63:     #   all non-tax adjustments.
64:     def total_before_tax
65:       amount + adjustments.reject(&:tax?).sum(&:amount)
66:     end
67: 
68:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this line item before VAT tax
69:     # @note just like `amount`, this does not include any additional tax
70:     def total_excluding_vat
71:       total_before_tax - included_tax_total
72:     end
73: 
74:     extend Spree::DisplayMoney
75: 
76:     money_methods :amount, :price,
77:       :included_tax_total, :additional_tax_total,
78:       :total, :total_before_tax, :total_excluding_vat
79: 
80:     # @return [Spree::Money] the price of this line item
81:     alias_method :money_price, :display_price
82:     alias_method :single_display_amount, :display_price
83:     alias_method :single_money, :display_price
84: 
85:     # @return [Spree::Money] the amount of this line item
86:     alias_method :money, :display_amount
87: 
88:     # Sets price from a `Spree::Money` object
89:     #
90:     # @param [Spree::Money] money - the money object to obtain price from
91:     def money_price=(money)
92:       if !money
93:         self.price = nil
94:       else
95:         self.price_currency = money.currency.iso_code
96:         self.price = money.to_d
97:       end
98:     end
99: 
100:     # @return [Boolean] true when it is possible to supply the required
101:     #   quantity of stock of this line item's variant
102:     def sufficient_stock?
103:       Spree::Config.stock.quantifier_class.new(variant).can_supply? quantity
104:     end
105: 
106:     # @return [Boolean] true when it is not possible to supply the required
107:     #   quantity of stock of this line item's variant
108:     def insufficient_stock?
109:       !sufficient_stock?
110:     end
111: 
112:     # Sets options on the line item and updates the price.
113:     #
114:     # The options can be arbitrary attributes on the LineItem.
115:     #
116:     # @param options [Hash] options for this line item
117:     def options=(options = {})
118:       return unless options.present?
119: 
120:       assign_attributes options
121: 
122:       # When price is part of the options we are not going to fetch
123:       # it from the variant. Please note that this always allows to set
124:       # a price for this line item, even if there is no existing price
125:       # for the associated line item in the order currency.
126:       unless options.key?(:price) || options.key?("price")
127:         self.money_price = variant.price_for_options(pricing_options)&.money
128:       end
129:     end
130: 
131:     # Recalculate the price using the pricing options for this line item.
132:     # Useful for making sure carts are up-to-date when prices change.
133:     # Will not make changes to completed orders.
134:     #
135:     def recalculate_price
136:       return if order.completed?
137:       self.money_price = variant.price_for_options(pricing_options)&.money
138:     end
139: 
140:     def pricing_options
141:       Spree::Config.pricing_options_class.from_line_item(self)
142:     end
143: 
144:     # @return [Spree::TaxCategory] the variant's tax category
145:     #
146:     # This returns the variant's tax category if the tax category ID on the line_item is nil. It looks
147:     # like an association, but really is an override.
148:     #
149:     def tax_category
150:       super || variant_tax_category
151:     end
152: 
153:     # @return [Integer] the variant's tax category ID
154:     #
155:     # This returns the variant's tax category ID if the tax category ID on the line_id is nil. It looks
156:     # like an association, but really is an override.
157:     #
158:     def tax_category_id
159:       super || variant_tax_category_id
160:     end
161: 
162:     private
163: 
164:     # Sets the quantity to zero if it is nil or less than zero.
165:     def normalize_quantity
166:       self.quantity = 0 if quantity.nil? || quantity < 0
167:     end
168: 
169:     # Sets tax category, price-related attributes from
170:     # its variant if they are nil and a variant is present.
171:     def set_required_attributes
172:       return if persisted?
173:       return unless variant
174:       self.tax_category ||= variant.tax_category
175:       set_pricing_attributes
176:     end
177: 
178:     # Set price, cost_price and currency.
179:     def set_pricing_attributes
180:       self.cost_price ||= variant.cost_price
181:       self.money_price = variant.price_for_options(pricing_options)&.money if price.nil?
182:       true
183:     end
184: 
185:     def update_inventory
186:       if (saved_changes? || target_shipment.present?) && order.has_checkout_step?("delivery")
187:         Spree::OrderInventory.new(order, self).verify(target_shipment)
188:       end
189:     end
190: 
191:     def destroy_inventory_units
192:       inventory_units.destroy_all
193:     end
194: 
195:     def price_match_order_currency
196:       return if price_currency.blank? || price_currency == currency
197: 
198:       errors.add(:price, :does_not_match_order_currency)
199:     end
200:   end
201: end

(End of file - total 201 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # An order's planned shipments including tracking and cost.
5:   #
6:   class Shipment < Spree::Base
7:     include Metadata
8: 
9:     belongs_to :order, class_name: "Spree::Order", touch: true, inverse_of: :shipments, optional: true
10:     belongs_to :stock_location, class_name: "Spree::StockLocation", optional: true
11: 
12:     has_many :adjustments, as: :adjustable, inverse_of: :adjustable, dependent: :delete_all, autosave: true
13:     has_many :inventory_units, dependent: :destroy, inverse_of: :shipment
14:     has_many :shipping_rates, -> { order(:cost) }, dependent: :destroy, inverse_of: :shipment, autosave: true
15:     has_many :shipping_methods, through: :shipping_rates
16:     has_many :state_changes, as: :stateful
17:     has_many :cartons, -> { distinct }, through: :inventory_units
18:     has_many :line_items, -> { distinct }, through: :inventory_units
19: 
20:     before_validation :set_cost_zero_when_nil
21: 
22:     before_destroy :ensure_can_destroy
23: 
24:     # TODO: remove the suppress_mailer temporary variable once we are calling 'ship'
25:     # from outside of the state machine and can actually pass variables through.
26:     attr_accessor :special_instructions, :suppress_mailer
27: 
28:     accepts_nested_attributes_for :inventory_units
29: 
30:     make_permalink field: :number, length: 11, prefix: "H"
31: 
32:     scope :pending, -> { with_state("pending") }
33:     scope :ready, -> { with_state("ready") }
34:     scope :shipped, -> { with_state("shipped") }
35:     scope :trackable, -> { where("tracking IS NOT NULL AND tracking != ''") }
36:     scope :with_state, ->(*state) { where(state:) }
37:     # sort by most recent shipped_at, falling back to created_at. add "id desc" to make specs that involve this scope more deterministic.
38:     scope :reverse_chronological, -> {
39:       order(Arel.sql("coalesce(#{Spree::Shipment.table_name}.shipped_at, #{Spree::Shipment.table_name}.created_at) desc"), id: :desc)
40:     }
41: 
42:     scope :by_store, ->(store) { joins(:order).merge(Spree::Order.by_store(store)) }
43: 
44:     include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.shipment
45: 
46:     self.allowed_ransackable_associations = ["order"]
47:     self.allowed_ransackable_attributes = ["number"]
48: 
49:     delegate :tax_category, :tax_category_id, to: :selected_shipping_rate, allow_nil: true
50: 
51:     def can_transition_from_pending_to_shipped?
52:       !requires_shipment?
53:     end
54: 
55:     def can_transition_from_pending_to_ready?
56:       order.can_ship? &&
57:         inventory_units.all? { |iu| iu.shipped? || iu.allow_ship? || iu.canceled? } &&
58:         (order.paid? || !Spree::Config[:require_payment_to_ship])
59:     end
60: 
61:     def can_transition_from_canceled_to_ready?
62:       can_transition_from_pending_to_ready?
63:     end
64: 
65:     extend DisplayMoney
66: 
67:     money_methods(
68:       :cost, :amount, :item_cost,
69:       :total, :total_before_tax
70:     )
71:     alias_attribute :amount, :cost
72: 
73:     def after_cancel
74:       manifest.each { |item| manifest_restock(item) }
75:     end
76: 
77:     def after_resume
78:       manifest.each { |item| manifest_unstock(item) }
79:     end
80: 
81:     def backordered?
82:       inventory_units.any?(&:backordered?)
83:     end
84: 
85:     def currency
86:       order ? order.currency : Spree::Config[:currency]
87:     end
88: 
89:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this shipment, taking into
90:     #   consideration all its adjustments.
91:     def total
92:       cost + adjustment_total
93:     end
94: 
95:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this item, taking into consideration
96:     #   all non-tax adjustments.
97:     def total_before_tax
98:       amount + adjustments.reject(&:tax?).sum(&:amount)
99:     end
100: 
101:     # @return [BigDecimal] the amount of this shipment before VAT tax
102:     # @note just like `cost`, this does not include any additional tax
103:     def total_excluding_vat
104:       total_before_tax - included_tax_total
105:     end
106: 
107:     def total_with_items
108:       total + item_cost
109:     end
110: 
111:     def editable_by?(_user)
112:       !shipped?
113:     end
114: 
115:     # Decrement the stock counts for all pending inventory units in this
116:     # shipment and mark.
117:     # Any previous non-pending inventory units are skipped as their stock had
118:     # already been allocated.
119:     def finalize!
120:       finalize_pending_inventory_units
121:     end
122: 
123:     def include?(variant)
124:       inventory_units_for(variant).present?
125:     end
126: 
127:     def inventory_units_for(variant)
128:       inventory_units.where(variant_id: variant.id)
129:     end
130: 
131:     def inventory_units_for_item(line_item, variant = nil)
132:       inventory_units.where(line_item_id: line_item.id, variant_id: line_item.variant.id || variant.id)
133:     end
134: 
135:     def item_cost
136:       line_items.sum(&:total)
137:     end
138: 
139:     def ready_or_pending?
140:       ready? || pending?
141:     end
142: 
143:     def refresh_rates
144:       return shipping_rates if shipped?
145:       return [] unless can_get_rates?
146: 
147:       # StockEstimator.new assigment below will replace the current shipping_method
148:       original_shipping_method_id = shipping_method.try!(:id)
149: 
150:       new_rates = Spree::Config.stock.estimator_class.new.shipping_rates(to_package)
151: 
152:       # If one of the new rates matches the previously selected shipping
153:       # method, select that instead of the default provided by the estimator.
154:       # Otherwise, keep the default.
155:       selected_rate = new_rates.detect { |rate| rate.shipping_method_id == original_shipping_method_id }
156:       if selected_rate
157:         new_rates.each do |rate|
158:           rate.selected = (rate == selected_rate)
159:         end
160:       end
161: 
162:       self.shipping_rates = new_rates
163:       save!
164: 
165:       shipping_rates
166:     end
167: 
168:     def select_shipping_method(shipping_method)
169:       estimator = Spree::Config.stock.estimator_class.new
170:       rates = estimator.shipping_rates(to_package, false)
171:       rate = rates.detect { |detected| detected.shipping_method_id == shipping_method.id }
172:       rate.selected = true
173:       self.shipping_rates = [rate]
174:     end
175: 
176:     def selected_shipping_rate
177:       shipping_rates.detect(&:selected?)
178:     end
179: 
180:     def manifest
181:       @manifest ||= Spree::ShippingManifest.new(inventory_units:).items
182:     end
183: 
184:     def selected_shipping_rate_id
185:       selected_shipping_rate.try(:id)
186:     end
187: 
188:     def selected_shipping_rate_id=(id)
189:       return if selected_shipping_rate_id == id
190:       new_rate = shipping_rates.detect { |rate| rate.id == id.to_i }
191:       unless new_rate
192:         fail(
193:           ArgumentError,
194:           "Could not find shipping rate id #{id} for shipment #{number}"
195:         )
196:       end
197: 
198:       transaction do
199:         selected_shipping_rate&.update!(selected: false)
200:         new_rate.update!(selected: true)
201:       end
202:     end
203: 
204:     def determine_state(order)
205:       Spree.deprecator.warn "Use Spree::Shipment#recalculate_state instead"
206: 
207:       return "shipped" if shipped?
208:       return "canceled" if order.canceled? || inventory_units.all?(&:canceled?)
209:       return "pending" unless order.can_ship?
210:       if can_transition_from_pending_to_ready?
211:         "ready"
212:       else
213:         "pending"
214:       end
215:     end
216: 
217:     # Assigns the appropriate +state+ according to the following logic:
218:     #
219:     # canceled   if order is canceled
220:     # pending    unless order is complete and +order.payment_state+ is +paid+
221:     # shipped    if already shipped (ie. does not change the state)
222:     # ready      all other cases
223:     def recalculate_state
224:       self.state =
225:         if shipped?
226:           "shipped"
227:         elsif inventory_units.none?
228:           "pending"
229:         elsif order.canceled? || inventory_units.all?(&:canceled?)
230:           "canceled"
231:         elsif !order.can_ship?
232:           "pending"
233:         elsif can_transition_from_pending_to_ready?
234:           "ready"
235:         else
236:           "pending"
237:         end
238:     end
239: 
240:     def set_up_inventory(state, variant, _order, line_item)
241:       inventory_units.create(
242:         state:,
243:         variant_id: variant.id,
244:         line_item_id: line_item.id
245:       )
246:     end
247: 
248:     def shipped=(value)
249:       return unless value == "1" && shipped_at.nil?
250:       self.shipped_at = Time.current
251:     end
252: 
253:     def shipping_method
254:       selected_shipping_rate.try(:shipping_method)
255:     end
256: 
257:     # Only one of either included_tax_total or additional_tax_total is set
258:     # This method returns the total of the two. Saves having to check if
259:     # tax is included or additional.
260:     def tax_total
261:       included_tax_total + additional_tax_total
262:     end
263: 
264:     def to_package
265:       package = Stock::Package.new(stock_location)
266:       package.shipment = self
267:       inventory_units.includes(variant: :product).joins(:variant).group_by(&:state).each do |state, state_inventory_units|
268:         package.add_multiple state_inventory_units, state.to_sym
269:       end
270:       package
271:     end
272: 
273:     def to_param
274:       number
275:     end
276: 
277:     def tracking_url
278:       return nil unless tracking && shipping_method
279: 
280:       @tracking_url ||= shipping_method.build_tracking_url(tracking)
281:     end
282: 
283:     def update_amounts
284:       assign_amounts
285:       persist_amounts
286:     end
287: 
288:     def assign_amounts
289:       return unless selected_shipping_rate
290:       self.cost = selected_shipping_rate.cost
291:     end
292: 
293:     def persist_amounts
294:       if cost_changed?
295:         update_columns(
296:           cost:,
297:           updated_at: Time.current
298:         )
299:       end
300:     end
301: 
302:     # Update Shipment and make sure Order states follow the shipment changes
303:     def update_attributes_and_order(params = {})
304:       if update(params)
305:         if params.key? :selected_shipping_rate_id
306:           # Changing the selected Shipping Rate won't update the cost (for now)
307:           # so we persist the Shipment#cost before running `order.recalculate`
308:           update_amounts
309:           order.recalculate
310:         end
311: 
312:         true
313:       end
314:     end
315: 
316:     # Updates the state of the Shipment bypassing any callbacks.
317:     #
318:     # If this moves the shipment to the 'shipped' state, after_ship will be
319:     # called.
320:     def update_state
321:       old_state = state
322:       new_state = recalculate_state
323:       if new_state != old_state
324:         update_columns state: new_state, updated_at: Time.current
325:         after_ship if new_state == "shipped"
326:       end
327:     end
328: 
329:     def requires_shipment?
330:       !stock_location || stock_location.fulfillable?
331:     end
332: 
333:     private
334: 
335:     def finalize_pending_inventory_units
336:       pending_units = inventory_units.select(&:pending?)
337:       Spree::Stock::InventoryUnitsFinalizer.new(pending_units).run!
338:     end
339: 
340:     def after_ship
341:       order.shipping.ship_shipment(self, suppress_mailer:)
342:     end
343: 
344:     def can_get_rates?
345:       order.ship_address&.valid?
346:     end
347: 
348:     def manifest_restock(item)
349:       if item.states["on_hand"].to_i > 0
350:         stock_location.restock item.variant, item.states["on_hand"], self
351:       end
352: 
353:       if item.states["backordered"].to_i > 0
354:         stock_location.restock_backordered item.variant, item.states["backordered"]
355:       end
356:     end
357: 
358:     def manifest_unstock(item)
359:       stock_location.unstock item.variant, item.quantity, self
360:     end
361: 
362:     def set_cost_zero_when_nil
363:       self.cost = 0 unless cost
364:     end
365: 
366:     def ensure_can_destroy
367:       if shipped? || canceled?
368:         errors.add(:state, :cannot_destroy, state:)
369:         throw :abort
370:       end
371:     end
372:   end
373: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Tracks the state of line items' fulfillment.
5:   #
6:   class InventoryUnit < Spree::Base
7:     PRE_SHIPMENT_STATES = %w[backordered on_hand]
8:     POST_SHIPMENT_STATES = %w[returned]
9:     CANCELABLE_STATES = ["on_hand", "backordered", "shipped"]
10: 
11:     belongs_to :variant, -> { with_discarded }, class_name: "Spree::Variant", inverse_of: :inventory_units, optional: true
12:     belongs_to :shipment, class_name: "Spree::Shipment", touch: true, inverse_of: :inventory_units, optional: true
13:     belongs_to :carton, class_name: "Spree::Carton", inverse_of: :inventory_units, optional: true
14:     belongs_to :line_item, class_name: "Spree::LineItem", inverse_of: :inventory_units, optional: true
15: 
16:     has_many :return_items, inverse_of: :inventory_unit, dependent: :destroy
17:     has_one :original_return_item, class_name: "Spree::ReturnItem", foreign_key: :exchange_inventory_unit_id, dependent: :destroy
18:     has_one :unit_cancel, class_name: "Spree::UnitCancel"
19:     has_one :order, through: :shipment
20: 
21:     delegate :order_id, to: :shipment
22: 
23:     def order=(_)
24:       raise "The order association has been removed from InventoryUnit. The order is now determined from the shipment."
25:     end
26: 
27:     validates :shipment, :line_item, :variant, presence: true
28: 
29:     before_destroy :ensure_can_destroy
30: 
31:     scope :pending, -> { where pending: true }
32:     scope :backordered, -> { where state: "backordered" }
33:     scope :on_hand, -> { where state: "on_hand" }
34:     scope :pre_shipment, -> { where(state: PRE_SHIPMENT_STATES) }
35:     scope :shipped, -> { where state: "shipped" }
36:     scope :post_shipment, -> { where(state: POST_SHIPMENT_STATES) }
37:     scope :returned, -> { where state: "returned" }
38:     scope :canceled, -> { where(state: "canceled") }
39:     scope :not_canceled, -> { where.not(state: "canceled") }
40:     scope :cancelable, -> { where(state: Spree::InventoryUnit::CANCELABLE_STATES, pending: false) }
41:     scope :backordered_per_variant, ->(stock_item) do
42:       includes(:shipment, :order)
43:         .where("spree_shipments.state != 'canceled'").references(:shipment)
44:         .where(variant_id: stock_item.variant_id)
45:         .where("spree_orders.completed_at is not null")
46:         .backordered.order(Spree::Order.arel_table[:completed_at].asc)
47:     end
48: 
49:     # @method backordered_for_stock_item(stock_item)
50:     # @param stock_item [Spree::StockItem] the stock item of the desired
51:     #   inventory units
52:     # @return [ActiveRecord::Relation<Spree::InventoryUnit>] backordered
53:     #   inventory units for the given stock item
54:     scope :backordered_for_stock_item, ->(stock_item) do
55:       backordered_per_variant(stock_item)
56:         .where(spree_shipments: {stock_location_id: stock_item.stock_location_id})
57:     end
58: 
59:     scope :shippable, -> { on_hand }
60: 
61:     include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.inventory_unit
62: 
63:     # @return [Spree::StockItem] the first stock item from this shipment's
64:     #   stock location that is associated with this inventory unit's variant
65:     def find_stock_item
66:       Spree::StockItem.where(stock_location_id: shipment.stock_location_id,
67:         variant_id:).first
68:     end
69: 
70:     # @return [Spree::ReturnItem] a valid return item for this inventory unit
71:     #   if one exists, or a new one if one does not
72:     def current_or_new_return_item
73:       Spree::ReturnItem.from_inventory_unit(self)
74:     end
75: 
76:     # @return [BigDecimal] the portion of the additional tax on the line item
77:     #   this inventory unit belongs to that is associated with this individual
78:     #   inventory unit
79:     def additional_tax_total
80:       line_item.additional_tax_total * percentage_of_line_item
81:     end
82: 
83:     # @return [BigDecimal] the portion of the included tax on the line item
84:     #   this inventory unit belongs to that is associated with this
85:     #   individual inventory unit
86:     def included_tax_total
87:       line_item.included_tax_total * percentage_of_line_item
88:     end
89: 
90:     # @return [Boolean] true if this inventory unit has any return items
91:     #   which have requested exchanges
92:     def exchange_requested?
93:       return_items.not_expired.any?(&:exchange_requested?)
94:     end
95: 
96:     def allow_ship?
97:       on_hand?
98:     end
99: 
100:     private
101: 
102:     def fulfill_order
103:       reload
104:       order.fulfill!
105:     end
106: 
107:     def percentage_of_line_item
108:       1 / BigDecimal(line_item.quantity)
109:     end
110: 
111:     def current_return_item
112:       return_items.not_cancelled.first
113:     end
114: 
115:     def ensure_can_destroy
116:       if !backordered? && !on_hand?
117:         errors.add(:state, :cannot_destroy, state:)
118:         throw :abort
119:       end
120: 
121:       if shipment.shipped? || shipment.canceled?
122:         errors.add(:base, :cannot_destroy_shipment_state, state: shipment.state)
123:         throw :abort
124:       end
125:     end
126:   end
127: end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Adjustments represent a change to the +item_total+ of an Order. Each
5:   # adjustment has an +amount+ that can be either positive or negative.
6:   #
7:   # Adjustments can be "unfinalized" or "finalized". Once an adjustment is finalized, it
8:   # will not be automatically updated.
9:   class Adjustment < Spree::Base
10:     belongs_to :adjustable, polymorphic: true, touch: true, optional: true
11:     belongs_to :source, polymorphic: true, optional: true
12:     belongs_to :order, class_name: "Spree::Order", inverse_of: false, optional: true
13:     belongs_to :adjustment_reason, class_name: "Spree::AdjustmentReason", inverse_of: :adjustments, optional: true
14: 
15:     validates :adjustable, presence: true
16:     validates :order, presence: true
17:     validates :label, presence: true
18:     validates :amount, numericality: true
19: 
20:     scope :not_finalized, -> { where(finalized: false) }
21:     scope :finalized, -> { where(finalized: true) }
22:     scope :cancellation, -> { where(source_type: "Spree::UnitCancel") }
23:     scope :tax, -> { where(source_type: "Spree::TaxRate") }
24:     scope :non_tax, -> do
25:       source_type = arel_table[:source_type]
26:       where(source_type.not_eq("Spree::TaxRate").or(source_type.eq(nil)))
27:     end
28:     scope :price, -> { where(adjustable_type: "Spree::LineItem") }
29:     scope :shipping, -> { where(adjustable_type: "Spree::Shipment") }
30:     scope :eligible, -> { all }
31:     class << self
32:       deprecate :eligible, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
33:     end
34:     scope :charge, -> { where("#{quoted_table_name}.amount >= 0") }
35:     scope :credit, -> { where("#{quoted_table_name}.amount < 0") }
36:     scope :nonzero, -> { where("#{quoted_table_name}.amount != 0") }
37:     scope :promotion, -> { where(source_type: Spree::Config.adjustment_promotion_source_types.map(&:to_s)) }
38:     scope :non_promotion, -> { where.not(source_type: Spree::Config.adjustment_promotion_source_types.map(&:to_s)) }
39:     scope :return_authorization, -> { where(source_type: "Spree::ReturnAuthorization") }
40:     scope :is_included, -> { where(included: true) }
41:     scope :additional, -> { where(included: false) }
42: 
43:     singleton_class.deprecate :return_authorization, deprecator: Spree.deprecator
44: 
45:     allowed_ransackable_attributes << "label"
46: 
47:     extend DisplayMoney
48: 
49:     money_methods :amount
50: 
51:     def finalize!
52:       update!(finalized: true)
53:     end
54: 
55:     def unfinalize!
56:       update!(finalized: false)
57:     end
58: 
59:     def finalize
60:       update(finalized: true)
61:     end
62: 
63:     def unfinalize
64:       update(finalized: false)
65:     end
66: 
67:     def currency
68:       adjustable ? adjustable.currency : Spree::Config[:currency]
69:     end
70: 
71:     # @return [Boolean] true when this is a promotion adjustment (Promotion adjustments have a {PromotionAction} source)
72:     def promotion?
73:       source_type.to_s.in?(Spree::Config.adjustment_promotion_source_types.map(&:to_s))
74:     end
75: 
76:     # @return [Boolean] true when this is a tax adjustment (Tax adjustments have a {TaxRate} source)
77:     def tax?
78:       source_type == "Spree::TaxRate"
79:     end
80: 
81:     # @return [Boolean] true when this is a cancellation adjustment (Cancellation adjustments have a {UnitCancel} source)
82:     def cancellation?
83:       source_type == "Spree::UnitCancel"
84:     end
85: 
86:     def eligible?
87:       true
88:     end
89:     alias_method :eligible, :eligible?
90:   end
91: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   module Stock
5:     class Package
6:       attr_reader :stock_location, :contents
7:       attr_accessor :shipment
8: 
9:       # @param stock_location [Spree::StockLocation] the stock location this package originates from
10:       # @param contents [Array<Spree::Stock::ContentItem>] the contents of this package
11:       def initialize(stock_location, contents = [])
12:         @stock_location = stock_location
13:         @contents = contents
14:       end
15: 
16:       # Adds an inventory unit to this package.
17:       #
18:       # @param inventory_unit [Spree::InventoryUnit] an inventory unit to be
19:       #   added to this package
20:       # @param state [:on_hand, :backordered] the state of the item to be
21:       #   added to this package
22:       def add(inventory_unit, state = :on_hand)
23:         contents << ContentItem.new(inventory_unit, state) unless find_item(inventory_unit)
24:       end
25: 
26:       # Adds multiple inventory units to this package.
27:       #
28:       # @param inventory_units [Array<Spree::InventoryUnit>] a collection of
29:       #   inventory units to be added to this package
30:       # @param state [:on_hand, :backordered] the state of the items to be
31:       #   added to this package
32:       def add_multiple(inventory_units, state = :on_hand)
33:         inventory_units.each { |inventory_unit| add(inventory_unit, state) }
34:       end
35: 
36:       # Removes a given inventory unit from this package.
37:       #
38:       # @param inventory_unit [Spree::InventoryUnit] the inventory unit to be
39:       #   removed from this package
40:       def remove(inventory_unit)
41:         item = find_item(inventory_unit)
42:         @contents -= [item] if item
43:       end
44: 
45:       # @return [Spree::Order] the order associated with this package
46:       def order
47:         # Fix regression that removed package.order.
48:         # Find it dynamically through an inventory_unit.
49:         contents.detect { |item| !!item.try(:line_item).try(:order) }.try(:line_item).try(:order)
50:       end
51: 
52:       # @return [Float] the summed weight of the contents of this package
53:       def weight
54:         contents.sum(&:weight)
55:       end
56: 
57:       # @return [Array<Spree::Stock::ContentItem>] the content items in this
58:       #   package which are on hand
59:       def on_hand
60:         contents.select(&:on_hand?)
61:       end
62: 
63:       # @return [Array<Spree::Stock::ContentItem>] the content items in this
64:       #   package which are backordered
65:       def backordered
66:         contents.select(&:backordered?)
67:       end
68: 
69:       # Find a content item in this package by inventory unit and optionally
70:       # state.
71:       #
72:       # @param inventory_unit [Spree::InventoryUnit] the desired inventory
73:       #   unit
74:       # @param state [:backordered, :on_hand, nil] the state of the desired
75:       #   content item, or nil for any state
76:       def find_item(inventory_unit, state = nil)
77:         contents.detect do |item|
78:           item.inventory_unit == inventory_unit &&
79:             (!state || item.state.to_s == state.to_s)
80:         end
81:       end
82: 
83:       # @param state [:backordered, :on_hand, nil] the state of the content
84:       #   items of which we want the quantity, or nil for the full quantity
85:       # @return [Fixnum] the number of inventory units in the package,
86:       #   counting only those in the given state if it was specified
87:       def quantity(state = nil)
88:         matched_contents = state.nil? ? contents : contents.select { |content| content.state.to_s == state.to_s }
89:         matched_contents.sum(&:quantity)
90:       end
91: 
92:       # @return [Boolean] true if there are no inventory units in this
93:       #   package
94:       def empty?
95:         quantity == 0
96:       end
97: 
98:       # @return [String] the currency of the order this package belongs to
99:       def currency
100:         order.currency
101:       end
102: 
103:       # @return [Array<Spree::ShippingCategory>] the shipping categories of the
104:       #   variants in this package
105:       def shipping_categories
106:         Spree::ShippingCategory.where(id: shipping_category_ids)
107:       end
108: 
109:       # @return [ActiveRecord::Relation] the [Spree::ShippingMethod]s available
110:       #   for this pacakge based on the stock location and shipping categories.
111:       def shipping_methods
112:         Spree::ShippingMethod
113:           .with_all_shipping_category_ids(shipping_category_ids)
114:           .available_in_stock_location(stock_location)
115:       end
116: 
117:       # @return [Spree::Shipment] a new shipment containing this package's
118:       #   inventory units, with the appropriate shipping rates and associated
119:       #   with the correct stock location
120:       def to_shipment
121:         # At this point we should only have one content item per inventory unit
122:         # across the entire set of inventory units to be shipped, which has
123:         # been taken care of by the Prioritizer
124:         contents.each { |content_item| content_item.inventory_unit.state = content_item.state.to_s }
125: 
126:         Spree::Shipment.new(
127:           order:,
128:           stock_location:,
129:           inventory_units: contents.map(&:inventory_unit)
130:         )
131:       end
132: 
133:       private
134: 
135:       # @return [Array<Fixnum>] the unique ids of all shipping categories of
136:       #   variants in this package
137:       def shipping_category_ids
138:         contents.map { |item| item.variant.shipping_category_id }.compact.uniq
139:       end
140:     end
141:   end
142: end

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/solidus/core/app/models/spree/stock/simple_coordinator.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   module Stock
5:     # A simple implementation of Stock Coordination
6:     #
7:     # The algorithm for allocating inventory is naive:
8:     #   * For each available Stock Location
9:     #     * Allocate as much on hand inventory as possible from this location
10:     #     * Remove the amount allocated from the amount desired
11:     #   * Repeat but for backordered inventory
12:     #   * Combine allocated and on hand inventory into a single shipment per-location
13:     #
14:     # Allocation logic can be changed using a custom class (as
15:     # configured in Spree::Config::stock_allocator_class )
16:     #
17:     # After allocation, splitters are run on each Package (as configured in
18:     # Spree::Config.environment.stock_splitters)
19:     #
20:     # Finally, shipping rates are calculated using the class configured as
21:     # Spree::Config.stock.estimator_class.
22:     class SimpleCoordinator
23:       attr_reader :order
24: 
25:       # @api private
26:       attr_reader :inventory_units, :splitters, :stock_locations,
27:         :filtered_stock_locations, :inventory_units_by_variant, :desired,
28:         :availability, :allocator, :packages
29: 
30:       def initialize(order, inventory_units_deprecated = nil, inventory_units: nil)
31:         if inventory_units_deprecated
32:           Spree.deprecator.warn "Using the `inventory_units` positional " \
33:             "argument is deprecated in favor of using the keyword argument. "
34: 
35:           inventory_units ||= inventory_units_deprecated
36:         end
37: 
38:         @order = order
39:         @inventory_units =
40:           inventory_units || Spree::Config.stock.inventory_unit_builder_class.new(order).units
41:         @splitters = Spree::Config.environment.stock_splitters
42: 
43:         @filtered_stock_locations = Spree::Config.stock.location_filter_class.new(load_stock_locations, order).filter
44:         sorted_stock_locations = Spree::Config.stock.location_sorter_class.new(filtered_stock_locations).sort
45:         @stock_locations = sorted_stock_locations
46: 
47:         @inventory_units_by_variant = @inventory_units.group_by(&:variant)
48:         @desired = Spree::StockQuantities.new(inventory_units_by_variant.transform_values(&:count))
49:         @availability = Spree::Stock::Availability.new(
50:           variants: desired.variants,
51:           stock_locations:
52:         )
53: 
54:         @allocator = Spree::Config.stock.allocator_class.new(availability)
55:       end
56: 
57:       def shipments
58:         @shipments ||= begin
59:           @packages = build_packages
60:           shipments = build_shipments
61: 
62:           # Make sure we don't add the proposed shipments to the order
63:           order.shipments = order.shipments - shipments
64: 
65:           shipments
66:         end
67:       end
68: 
69:       private
70: 
71:       def load_stock_locations
72:         Spree::StockLocation.all
73:       end
74: 
75:       def build_shipments
76:         # Turn the Stock::Packages into a Shipment with rates
77:         packages.map do |package|
78:           shipment = package.shipment = package.to_shipment
79:           shipment.shipping_rates = Spree::Config.stock.estimator_class.new.shipping_rates(package)
80:           shipment
81:         end
82:       end
83: 
84:       def build_packages
85:         # Allocate any available on hand inventory and remaining desired inventory from backorders
86:         on_hand_packages, backordered_packages, leftover = allocator.allocate_inventory(desired)
87: 
88:         raise Spree::Order::InsufficientStock.new(items: leftover.quantities) unless leftover.empty?
89: 
90:         packages = stock_locations.map do |stock_location|
91:           # Combine on_hand and backorders into a single package per-location
92:           on_hand = on_hand_packages[stock_location.id] || Spree::StockQuantities.new
93:           backordered = backordered_packages[stock_location.id] || Spree::StockQuantities.new
94: 
95:           # Skip this location it has no inventory
96:           next if on_hand.empty? && backordered.empty?
97: 
98:           # Turn our raw quantities into a Stock::Package
99:           package = Spree::Stock::Package.new(stock_location)
100:           package.add_multiple(get_units(on_hand), :on_hand)
101:           package.add_multiple(get_units(backordered), :backordered)
102: 
103:           package
104:         end.compact
105: 
106:         # Split the packages
107:         split_packages(packages)
108:       end
109: 
110:       def split_packages(initial_packages)
111:         initial_packages.flat_map do |initial_package|
112:           stock_location = initial_package.stock_location
113:           Spree::Stock::SplitterChain.new(stock_location, @splitters).split([initial_package])
114:         end
115:       end
116: 
117:       def get_units(quantities)
118:         # Change our raw quantities back into inventory units
119:         quantities.flat_map do |variant, quantity|
120:           inventory_units_by_variant[variant].shift(quantity)
121:         end
122:       end
123:     end
124:   end
125: end

</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Models the return of Inventory Units to a Stock Location for an Order.
5:   #
6:   class ReturnAuthorization < Spree::Base
7:     include Metadata
8: 
9:     belongs_to :order, class_name: "Spree::Order", inverse_of: :return_authorizations, optional: true
10: 
11:     has_many :return_items, inverse_of: :return_authorization, dependent: :destroy
12:     has_many :inventory_units, through: :return_items, dependent: :nullify
13:     has_many :customer_returns, through: :return_items
14: 
15:     belongs_to :stock_location, optional: true
16:     belongs_to :reason, class_name: "Spree::ReturnReason", foreign_key: :return_reason_id, optional: true
17: 
18:     before_create :generate_number
19: 
20:     accepts_nested_attributes_for :return_items, allow_destroy: true
21: 
22:     validates :order, presence: true
23:     validates :stock_location, presence: true
24:     validate :must_have_shipped_units, on: :create
25:     validate :no_previously_exchanged_inventory_units, on: :create
26: 
27:     include ::Spree::Config.state_machines.return_authorization
28: 
29:     extend DisplayMoney
30: 
31:     money_methods :amount, :total_excluding_vat
32: 
33:     self.allowed_ransackable_attributes = ["memo"]
34: 
35:     def total_excluding_vat
36:       return_items.sum(&:total_excluding_vat)
37:     end
38: 
39:     def amount
40:       return_items.sum(:amount)
41:     end
42: 
43:     def currency
44:       order.currency
45:     end
46: 
47:     def refundable_amount
48:       order.item_total_before_tax + order.promo_total
49:     end
50: 
51:     def customer_returned_items?
52:       customer_returns.exists?
53:     end
54: 
55:     def can_cancel_return_items?
56:       return_items.any?(&:can_cancel?) || return_items.blank?
57:     end
58: 
59:     private
60: 
61:     def must_have_shipped_units
62:       if order.nil? || order.inventory_units.shipped.none?
63:         errors.add(:order, I18n.t("spree.has_no_shipped_units"))
64:       end
65:     end
66: 
67:     def generate_number
68:       self.number ||= loop do
69:         random = "RA#{Array.new(9) { rand(9) }.join}"
70:         break random unless self.class.exists?(number: random)
71:       end
72:     end
73: 
74:     def no_previously_exchanged_inventory_units
75:       if return_items.map(&:inventory_unit).any?(&:exchange_requested?)
76:         errors.add(:base, I18n.t("spree.return_items_cannot_be_created_for_inventory_units_that_are_already_awaiting_exchange"))
77:       end
78:     end
79: 
80:     def cancel_return_items
81:       return_items.each { |item| item.cancel! if item.can_cancel? }
82:     end
83:   end
84: end

14:         dependent: :destroy,
15:         inverse_of: :order
16:       base.has_many :solidus_promotions, through: :solidus_order_promotions, source: :promotion
17:     end

19:     def discountable_item_total
20:       line_items.sum(&:discountable_amount)
21:     end
22: 
23:     def reset_current_discounts
24:       line_items.each(&:reset_current_discounts)
25:       shipments.each(&:reset_current_discounts)
26:     end
27: 
28:     # This helper method excludes line items that are managed by an order benefit for the benefit
29:     # of calculators and benefits that discount normal line items. Line items that are managed by an
30:     # order benefits handle their discounts themselves.
31:     def discountable_line_items
32:       line_items.reject(&:managed_by_order_benefit)
33:     end
34: 
35:     def free_from_order_benefit?(line_item, _options)

37:     end

42:       rescue
43:         nil
44:       end
45:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module SolidusLegacyPromotions
4:   module SpreeOrderUpdaterPatch
5:     def update_adjustment_total
6:       update_adjustments
7: 
8:       all_items = (line_items + shipments).reject(&:marked_for_destruction?)
9:       order_tax_adjustments = adjustments.select(&:eligible?).select(&:tax?)
10: 
11:       order.adjustment_total = all_items.sum(&:adjustment_total) + adjustments.select(&:eligible?).sum(&:amount)
12:       order.included_tax_total = all_items.sum(&:included_tax_total) + order_tax_adjustments.select(&:included?).sum(&:amount)
13:       order.additional_tax_total = all_items.sum(&:additional_tax_total) + order_tax_adjustments.reject(&:included?).sum(&:amount)
14: 
15:       recalculate_order_total
16:     end
17: 
18:     def recalculate_item_totals
19:       [*line_items, *shipments].each do |item|
20:         Spree::Config.item_total_class.new(item).recalculate!
21: 
22:         # The cancellation_total isn't persisted anywhere but is included in
23:         # the adjustment_total.
24:         #
25:         # Core doesn't have "eligible" adjustments anymore, so we need to
26:         # override the adjustment_total calculation to exclude them for legacy
27:         # promotions.
28:         item.adjustment_total = item.adjustments.select { |adjustment|
29:           adjustment.eligible? &&
30:             !adjustment.marked_for_destruction? &&
31:             !adjustment.included?
32:         }.sum(&:amount)
33: 
34:         next unless item.changed?
35: 
36:         item.assign_attributes(
37:           promo_total: item.promo_total,
38:           included_tax_total: item.included_tax_total,
39:           additional_tax_total: item.additional_tax_total,
40:           adjustment_total: item.adjustment_total
41:         )
42:       end
43:     end
44:     Spree::OrderUpdater.prepend self
45:   end
46: end

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/solidus/core/app/models/spree/fulfilment_changer.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Service class to change fulfilment of inventory units of a particular variant
5:   # to another shipment. The other shipment would typically have a different
6:   # shipping method, stock location or delivery date, such that we actually change
7:   # the planned fulfilment for the items in question.
8:   #
9:   # Can be used to merge shipments by moving all items to another shipment, because
10:   # this class will delete any empty original shipment.
11:   #
12:   # @attr [Spree::Shipment] current_shipment The shipment we transfer units from
13:   # @attr [Spree::Shipment] desired_shipment The shipment we want to move units onto
14:   # @attr [Spree::StockLocation] current_stock_location The stock location of the current shipment
15:   # @attr [Spree::StockLocation] desired_stock_location The stock location of the desired shipment
16:   # @attr [Spree::Variant] variant We only move units that represent this variant
17:   # @attr [Integer] quantity How many units we want to move
18:   #
19:   class FulfilmentChanger
20:     include ActiveModel::Validations
21: 
22:     attr_accessor :current_shipment, :desired_shipment
23:     attr_reader :variant, :quantity, :current_stock_location, :desired_stock_location, :track_inventory
24: 
25:     def initialize(current_shipment:, desired_shipment:, variant:, quantity:, track_inventory:)
26:       @current_shipment = current_shipment
27:       @desired_shipment = desired_shipment
28:       @current_stock_location = current_shipment.stock_location
29:       @desired_stock_location = desired_shipment.stock_location
30:       @variant = variant
31:       @quantity = quantity
32:       @track_inventory = track_inventory
33:     end
34: 
35:     validates :quantity, numericality: {greater_than: 0}
36:     validate :current_shipment_not_already_shipped
37:     validate :desired_shipment_different_from_current
38:     validates :desired_stock_location, presence: true
39:     validate :enough_stock_at_desired_location, if: :handle_stock_counts?
40: 
41:     # Performs the change of fulfilment
42:     # @return [true, false] Whether the requested fulfilment change was successful
43:     def run!
44:       # Validations here are intended to catch all necessary prerequisites.
45:       # We return early so all checks have happened already.
46:       return false if invalid?
47:       desired_shipment.save! if desired_shipment.new_record?
48: 
49:       if track_inventory
50:         run_tracking_inventory
51:       else
52:         run_without_tracking_inventory
53:       end
54: 
55:       # We modified the inventory units at the database level for speed reasons.
56:       # The downside of that is that we need to reload the associations.
57:       current_shipment.inventory_units.reload
58:       desired_shipment.inventory_units.reload
59: 
60:       # If the current shipment now has no inventory units left, we won't need it any longer.
61:       if current_shipment.inventory_units.length.zero?
62:         current_shipment.destroy!
63:       else
64:         # The current shipment has changed, so we need to make sure that shipping rates
65:         # have the correct amount.
66:         current_shipment.refresh_rates
67:       end
68: 
69:       # The desired shipment has also change, so we need to make sure shipping rates
70:       # are up-to-date, too.
71:       desired_shipment.refresh_rates
72: 
73:       # In order to reflect the changes in the order totals
74:       desired_shipment.order.reload
75:       desired_shipment.order.recalculate
76: 
77:       true
78:     end
79: 
80:     private
81: 
82:     # When moving things from one stock location to another, we need to restock items
83:     # from the current location and unstock them at the desired location.
84:     # Also, when we want to track inventory changes, we need to make sure that we have
85:     # enough stock at the desired location to fulfil the order. Based on how many items
86:     # we can take from the desired location, we could end up with some items being backordered.
87:     def run_tracking_inventory
88:       # Retrieve how many on hand items we can take from desired stock location
89:       available_quantity = get_available_quantity
90:       new_on_hand_quantity = [available_quantity, quantity].min
91:       backordered_quantity = get_backordered_quantity(available_quantity, new_on_hand_quantity)
92: 
93:       # Determine how many backordered and on_hand items we'll need to move. We
94:       # don't want to move more than what's being asked. And we can't move a
95:       # negative amount, which is why we need to perform our min/max logic here.
96:       backordered_quantity_to_move = [backordered_quantity, quantity].min
97:       on_hand_quantity_to_move = [quantity - backordered_quantity_to_move, 0].max
98: 
99:       ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
100:         if handle_stock_counts?
101:           # We only run this query if we need it.
102:           current_on_hand_quantity = [current_shipment.inventory_units.pre_shipment.size, quantity].min
103:           unstock_quantity = desired_shipment.stock_location.backorderable?(variant) ? quantity : new_on_hand_quantity
104: 
105:           # Restock things we will not fulfil from the current shipment anymore
106:           current_stock_location.restock(variant, current_on_hand_quantity, current_shipment)
107:           # Unstock what we will fulfil with the new shipment
108:           desired_stock_location.unstock(variant, unstock_quantity, desired_shipment)
109:         end
110: 
111:         # These two statements are the heart of this class. We change the number
112:         # of inventory units requested from one shipment to the other.
113:         # We order by state, because `'backordered' < 'on_hand'`.
114:         # We start to move the new actual backordered quantity, so the remaining
115:         # quantity can be set to on_hand state.
116:         current_shipment
117:           .inventory_units
118:           .where(variant:)
119:           .order(state: :asc)
120:           .limit(backordered_quantity_to_move)
121:           .update_all(shipment_id: desired_shipment.id, state: :backordered)
122: 
123:         current_shipment
124:           .inventory_units
125:           .where(variant:)
126:           .order(state: :asc)
127:           .limit(on_hand_quantity_to_move)
128:           .update_all(shipment_id: desired_shipment.id, state: :on_hand)
129:       end
130:     end
131: 
132:     # When we don't track inventory, we can just move the inventory units from one shipment
133:     # to the other.
134:     def run_without_tracking_inventory
135:       ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do
136:         current_shipment
137:           .inventory_units
138:           .where(variant:)
139:           .order(state: :asc)
140:           .limit(quantity)
141:           .update_all(shipment_id: desired_shipment.id)
142:       end
143:     end
144: 
145:     # We don't need to handle stock counts for incomplete orders. Also, if
146:     # the new shipment and the desired shipment will ship from the same stock location,
147:     # unstocking and restocking will not be necessary.
148:     def handle_stock_counts?
149:       current_shipment.order.completed? && current_stock_location != desired_stock_location
150:     end
151: 
152:     def get_available_quantity
153:       if current_stock_location != desired_stock_location
154:         desired_location_quantifier.positive_stock
155:       else
156:         sl_availability = current_location_quantifier.positive_stock
157:         shipment_availability = current_shipment.inventory_units.where(variant: variant).on_hand.count
158:         sl_availability + shipment_availability
159:       end
160:     end
161: 
162:     def get_backordered_quantity(available_quantity, new_on_hand_quantity)
163:       if current_stock_location != desired_stock_location
164:         quantity - new_on_hand_quantity
165:       else
166:         shipment_quantity = current_shipment.inventory_units.where(variant: variant).size
167:         shipment_quantity - available_quantity
168:       end
169:     end
170: 
171:     def current_shipment_not_already_shipped
172:       if current_shipment.shipped?
173:         errors.add(:current_shipment, :has_already_been_shipped)
174:       end
175:     end
176: 
177:     def enough_stock_at_desired_location
178:       unless desired_location_quantifier.can_supply?(quantity)
179:         errors.add(:desired_shipment, :not_enough_stock_at_desired_location)
180:       end
181:     end
182: 
183:     def desired_location_quantifier
184:       @desired_location_quantifier ||= Spree::Config.stock.quantifier_class.new(variant, desired_stock_location)
185:     end
186: 
187:     def current_location_quantifier
188:       @current_location_quantifier ||= Spree::Config.stock.quantifier_class.new(variant, current_stock_location)
189:     end
190: 
191:     def desired_shipment_different_from_current
192:       if desired_shipment.id == current_shipment.id
193:         errors.add(:desired_shipment, :can_not_transfer_within_same_shipment)
194:       end
195:     end
196:   end
197: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   module TestingSupport
5:     class OrderWalkthrough
6:       def self.up_to(state, user: nil)
7:         new.up_to(state, user:)
8:       end
9: 
10:       def up_to(state, user: nil)
11:         # Need to create a valid zone too...
12:         @zone = ::FactoryBot.create(:zone)
13:         @country = Spree::Country.find_by(iso: "US") || ::FactoryBot.create(:country)
14:         @state = ::FactoryBot.create(:state, country: @country)
15: 
16:         @zone.members << Spree::ZoneMember.create(zoneable: @country)
17: 
18:         # A shipping method must exist for rates to be displayed on checkout page
19:         ::FactoryBot.create(:shipping_method, zones: [@zone]).tap do |sm|
20:           sm.calculator.preferred_amount = 10
21:           sm.calculator.preferred_currency = Spree::Config[:currency]
22:           sm.calculator.save
23:         end
24: 
25:         order = Spree::Order.create!(
26:           user:,
27:           email: "solidus@example.com",
28:           store: Spree::Store.first || ::FactoryBot.create(:store)
29:         )
30:         add_line_item!(order)
31:         order.next!
32: 
33:         states_to_process = if state == :complete
34:           states
35:         else
36:           end_state_position = states.index(state.to_sym)
37:           states[..end_state_position]
38:         end
39: 
40:         states_to_process.each do |state_to_process|
41:           send(state_to_process, order)
42:         end
43: 
44:         order
45:       end
46: 
47:       private
48: 
49:       def add_line_item!(order)
50:         ::FactoryBot.create(:line_item, order:)
51:         order.reload
52:       end
53: 
54:       def address(order)
55:         order.bill_address = ::FactoryBot.create(:address, country: @country, state: @state)
56:         order.ship_address = ::FactoryBot.create(:address, country: @country, state: @state)
57:         order.next!
58:       end
59: 
60:       def delivery(order)
61:         order.next!
62:       end
63: 
64:       def payment(order)
65:         credit_card = ::FactoryBot.create(:credit_card, user: order.user)
66:         order.payments.create!(payment_method: credit_card.payment_method, amount: order.total, source: credit_card)
67:         # TODO: maybe look at some way of making this payment_state change automatic
68:         order.payment_state = "paid"
69:         order.next!
70:       end
71: 
72:       def confirm(order)
73:         order.complete!
74:       end
75: 
76:       def complete(order)
77:         # noop?
78:       end
79: 
80:       def states
81:         [:address, :delivery, :payment, :confirm]
82:       end
83:     end
84:   end
85: end

139:     def update_spree_roles(given_roles, ability:)
